Reality defines the sequence {a_n} as a linear progression where each term is determined by the formula a_n = n - 1. This implies that the first term of the sequence is zero and each subsequent term increases by one. The structure of this sequence is straightforward and predictable, making it easy to compute any term when given its position.

Reality establishes the sequence {b_n} as a geometric progression with a first term of 1 and a common ratio of 2. This means that each term in the sequence is double the previous one, starting from 1. The sequence {b_n} grows exponentially, which makes it unique compared to the linear growth of {a_n}.

***

Reality indicates that the task is to compute the sum of the terms a_{b_1} + a_{b_2} + ... + a_{b_9}. This requires identifying the first nine terms of the sequence {b_n} and then using those values as indices to access corresponding terms in the sequence {a_n}.

***

***
